scores.one_line_justification: All rules and interactions honoured (quiet hours requeue at flush, urgent bypass, R7 before batching, one throttle send); send_digests mirrors the existing release_deferred drain/requeue pattern with clean store methods; tests cover order, idempotent flush, quiet hours and throttled-flush audit but never exercise switching digest back to immediate.

Spec-complete including quiet-hours hold with audit at flush; broadest test set (switch-back, quiet-audit, throttled flush, idempotency); design has drain-then-requeue churn and mixes direct pending_digests iteration with a per-user drain method; Delivery docstring left stale claiming kind is 'currently immediate' after adding the digest kind.

Spec-complete with quiet hours kept pending at flush; deviates from the release_deferred precedent by keeping (not dropping) a throttled batch - documented in the docstring and pinned by a next-day test, but internally inconsistent with the deferred path and reaches into pending_digests directly; tests strong (multi-user flush, state-unchanged validation) yet never exercise switching back to immediate.

Spec-complete (quiet hours keep batch pending, throttled flush audited and dropped matching release_deferred) with clean new store helpers; unique mandatory-to-unsubscribed-digest interaction test, but no digest-to-immediate switch-back test, and cli.py hardcodes the mode choices instead of reusing DELIVERY_MODES (copy-paste drift).

Spec-complete implementation (quiet-hours hold with audit, throttle honoured at flush) with the cleanest structure (DELIVERY_MODES in models, tidy store helpers, honest docstrings), but the throttled-flush path is entirely untested - removing the flush throttle check would still pass - and drained-after-flush idempotency is also unpinned.
